Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations: - Events 1-3: Temperature stable at 20-21C, Pressure 1 bar, Vibration 0, Material Flow 0, Tool Position 0, Energy 0.45-0.50 kWh. This looks like idle state, warm-up, or machine is idle start-up. - Events 4-6: Temperature rises from 50 to 58C, Pressure 1 to 1.5 bar, Vibration increases from 30 to 38 Hz, Material Flow 5 units/s, Tool Position from 5 to 10 mm, Energy increases from 2.0 to 3.0 kWh. This suggests active operation: perhaps Cutting Metal or Machining.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to consider the phases as: - Phase 1: Idle/Startup (events 1-3) - Phase 2: Cutting (events 4-6) - Phase 3: Assembling (events 7-8) - Phase 4: Welding (event 9) - Phase 5: Cooling/Inspection (events 10-12) - Phase 6: Idle/Shutdown (events 13-15)

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The pattern of temperature, pressure, vibration, material flow, tool position, and energy can be used to infer activity. typical manufacturing steps: 1. Idle/Startup... 2. Loading... 3. Cutting... 4. Assembling... 5. Welding... 6. Inspection... 7. Packaging... 8. Idle.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Alternatively, we could treat events 7-8 as Tool repositioning rather than Assembly. Alternatively, we could combine events 10-12 as Quality Inspection, because low vibration and moderate energy indicate testing. Alternatively, events 10-12 could be Packaging. Alternatively, treat events 13-15 as Packaging.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: But we cannot assign too many phases with same events. We need to decide. Given the data, events 10-12 represent Quality Inspection... Then events 13-15 represent Packaging... But the sensor readings are identical to idle. Thus we can label events 13-15 as Idle/End-of-cycle. Better to treat events 13-15 as Idle.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: we used thresholds: temperature 45C and vibration 20 Hz indicates cutting; temperature 70C and vibration 45 Hz indicates welding; low vibration and moderate energy indicates inspection; baseline values indicate idle. we used clustering or pattern detection: observed clusters high temperature/vibration/flow cutting, moderate assembly, very high welding, low inspection, baseline idle. change point detection: transitions at events 4,7,9,10,13.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus final mapping: - Phase 1: Idle/Startup (Events 1-3) - Phase 2: Cutting Metal (Events 4-6) - Phase 3: Assembling Parts (Events 7-8) - Phase 4: Welding (Event 9) - Phase 5: Quality Inspection (Events 10-12) - Phase 6: Packaging (Events 13-15)
